g.text: PROJECT AND IMPORTANCE OF PROJECT MANAGEMENT

g.data-narration: A project is a temporary endeavor to create something unique, with a definite beginning and end.
g.text: A temporary endeavor undertaken to create a unique product, service, or result.
• Temporary (definite start/end) • Unique deliverables • Progressive elaboration

g.data-narration: Projects are temporary and unique, while operations are ongoing and repetitive activities.
Projects have four key characteristics: temporary nature, unique deliverables, progressive elaboration, and limited resources.
Project stakeholders include sponsors, managers, teams, customers, and external parties who are affected by or affect the project.
The project management triangle shows that scope, time, and cost are interconnected - changing one affects the others.
Project management has five process groups: Initiating, Planning, Executing, Monitoring & Controlling, and Closing.
The project life cycle has four phases: Concept, Development, Implementation, and Termination.
Project management helps identify and manage risks before they become problems, preventing project failure.
Other key benefits include customer satisfaction, cost control, and effective communication among stakeholders.
SDLC focuses on technical phases while project management provides the framework for managing these activities effectively.
Example: An e-commerce platform project has defined scope, timeline, budget, and requires proper project management for success.
Remember these key terms: WBS, milestone, deliverables, and baseline are essential vocabulary in project management.
In summary, project management is essential for software engineering success through proper planning, execution, and control.
g.text: Projects are temporary unique endeavors requiring proper management
Five Process Groups: Initiating → Planning → Executing → Monitoring → Closing
Iron Triangle: Scope × Time × Cost = Quality
